LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS YENEFER FERNANDO AGUILAR AND DILIA CASTRO'S MOTION TO QUASH PRODUCTION OF BUSINESS RECORDS AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS TRANSFERRED TO DEPARTMENT 501, THE HOUSING COURT TO BE HEARD ON THE SAME DAY AND TIME. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: G. GONZALES; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER CSR#6576, EMAIL: RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG.

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFFS YENEFER FERNANDO AGUILAR AND DILIA CASTRO'S MOTION TO QUASH PRODUCTION OF BUSINESS RECORDS AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART AS FOLLOWS: THE SUBPOENA IS MODIFIED TO PROVIDE FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS OR PORTIONS THEREOF THAT RELATE TO COMPLAINTS OR TREATMENT FOR EMOTIONAL DISTRESS FROM JANUARY 2010 TO THE PRESENT, INCLUDING ANY DOCUMENTS RELATED TO THE THERAPY GROUP AND SOCIAL WORKER VISIT REFERRED TO IN CASTRO'S RESPONSE TO INTERROGATORY 6.4. QUEST SHALL PROVIDE TO PLAINTIFFS' COUNSEL, JESSICA ALEXANDER, COPY OF THE DOCUMENTS QUEST RECEIVED AS A RESULT OF THE SUBPOENA, WHICH ARE TO BE NUMBERED, AND QUEST SHALL RETAIN THE ORIGINALS. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SHALL INSPECT THE DOCUMENTS TO DETERMINE IF ANY DOCUMENTS OR PORTION THEREOF ARE OUTSIDE THE SCOPE OF THE MODIFIED SUBPOENA. WITHIN FIFTEEN DAYS OF RECEIPT OF THE DOCUMENTS FROM QUEST, PLAINTIFFS' COUNSEL SHALL PROVIDE DEFENSE COUNSEL, WITH THOSE DOCUMENTS THAT COME WITHIN THE SCOPE OF THE MODIFIED SUBPOENA, INCLUDING ANY NECESSARY REDACTIONS FOR PRIVILEGED MATERIAL, TOGETHER WITH A WITH A PRIVILEGED LOG FOR ANY DOCUMENTS WITHHELD OR REDACTED. IF DEFENSE COUNSEL BELIEVES THAT ADDITIONAL DOCUMENTS SHOULD HAVE BEEN PRODUCED OR THAT INAPPROPRIATE REDACTIONS WERE MADE THEN DEFENDANTS MAY BRING A NEW MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES. PLAINTIFFS' COUNSEL SHALL ALSO RETAIN A COPY OF ALL RECORDS PROVIDED BY QUEST WITHOUT REDACTIONS. THE REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: A. PASCUAL, NOT REPORTED (D501)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-04-2018 Continued To Sep-10-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiffs Form Interrogatories-General, Special Interrogatories, Requests For Production Of Documents And Requests For Admission; And To Compel Compliance Pursuant To Ccp 2031.320, Subd. (A); And For An Award Of Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-15-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jessica Alexandra, Esq. T:415.500.6089, for plaintiff/moving party;
Mark W. Allen, Esq. of Bledsoe Diestel Treppa & Crane LLP, T:415.981.5411, for defendant/opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling with modification on PLAINTIFFS YENEFER FERNANDO AGUILAR, DILIA CASTRO's Motion To Compel Defendants To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiffs' Form Interrogatories-General, Special Interrogatories, Requests For Production Of Documents And Requests For Admission; And To Compel Compliance Pursuant To CCP 2031.320, Subd. (A); And For An Award Of Sanctions, as follows:

Plaintiff's motion is granted as to special interrogatories nos. 11 and 12. The remainder of plaintiffs' motion is granted in part and denied in part. It is granted as to form interrogatories 3.6, 3.7, and 17.1 (in reference to request for admission 14), and defendants' objections to form interrogatory 17.1 (in reference to request for admission 14) are overruled. The motion is also granted as to requests for production 1, 16, and 18. Within 30 days of this order, defendants shall provide amended, verified, code-compliant responses to these interrogatories, produce all responsive documents, and provide a privilege log for any documents withheld on the basis of asserting a claim of privilege. The motion is denied as to form interrogatories 12.2 and 12.3 without prejudice to plaintiffs serving new form interrogatories to include plaintiffs' definition of "INCIDENT." The motion is denied as to requests for admission 7, 8, and 9; special interrogatory 20; and request for production 6 because of ambiguity and confusion as to the term "CONDITIONS." Plaintiffs' request for sanctions is denied because defendants had substantial justification in opposing this motion.

PLaintiffs to prepare a proposed order that repeats the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge Pro Tem: Melinda Derish; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.

Plaintiffs' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Production Of Business Records And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-11-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The tentative ruling is adopted. PLAINTIFFS YENEFER FERNANDO AGUILAR and DILIA CASTRO'S Motion To Quash Production Of Business Records And Request For Sanctions is TRANSFERRED to Department 501, the Housing Court to be heard on the same day and time. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter CSR#6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org.

Plaintiffs Yenefer Fernando Aguilar And Dilia Castro'S Motion To Quash Production Of Business Records And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-11-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiffs Yenefer Fernando Aguilar And Dilia Castro'S Motion To Quash Production Of Business Records And Request For Sanctions is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part as follows: the subpoena is modified to provide for production of documents or portions thereof that relate to complaints or treatment for emotional distress from January 2010 to the present, including any documents related to the therapy group and social worker visit referred to in Castro's response to interrogatory 6.4. Quest shall provide to Plaintiffs' counsel, Jessica Alexander, copy of the documents Quest received as a result of the subpoena, which are to be numbered, and Quest shall retain the originals. Plaintiff's counsel shall inspect the documents to determine if any documents or portion thereof are outside the scope of the modified subpoena. Within fifteen days of receipt of the documents from Quest, Plaintiffs' counsel shall provide defense counsel, with those documents that come within the scope of the modified subpoena, including any necessary redactions for privileged material, together with a with a privileged log for any documents withheld or redacted. If defense counsel believes that additional documents should have been produced or that inappropriate redactions were made then defendants may bring a new motion to compel further responses. Plaintiffs' counsel shall also retain a copy of all records provided by Quest without redactions. The requests for sanctions are denied. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).
Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: A. Pascual, Not reported (D501)
